families.hypothesis: For left-origin lateral threats, rightward drift should dominate only when TTC is very short and the right pedestrian gap is adequate; when the escape-side pedestrian gap is small, the law should suppress drift-right and prefer hard braking/hold-lane despite the lateral threat.

trace.analysis: No new rule is proposed for family left-crossing-right-ped-gap because the swept grid contains 0 lawful points and 27 ambiguous points. Requirement 3 requires at least 3 supporting lawful points per rule, and requirement 1 requires numeric guard bounds to be supported by lawful grid points. Since every point is ambiguous with all maneuver severities tied at 4, the grid does not provide lawful evidence for a dominant maneuver or for safe numeric widening. Existing rule seed-tdrift-right is not contradicted here because its guard excludes pedestrian_side=right, matching the intended suppression of rightward drift when a right-side pedestrian is present. Existing seed-tdrift-left is not contradicted by this grid because its relevant guard is for front_right/right approaches with lateral_speed_mps>=4 and on_collision_course=true; the front_right entries in this grid appear to be non-collision/defaulted observations with ttc_s=99 and lateral_speed_mps=0, so they do not support tightening or deprecation. Existing seed-brake-sufficient is also not contradicted: code 0 is among the tied minimum-severity maneuvers at all ambiguous front/oncoming-looking points, but ambiguity prevents creating or widening a braking rule. The mock lateral-crossing rule has pedestrian_side=none and therefore does not overlap this right-pedestrian scenario.
